meta.libraryPositioning: Editable SVG templates curated for the TechnicalRoute engine. Each template is a structural starting point — the agent picks one based on the Diagram Contract's archetype + sub_variant + content shape, then injects the paper's terms / data. Templates here are the FIRST choice (vector, editable in PowerPoint/WPS). Only when no template fits does the engine fall back to AI image generation (raster).
meta.summaryGrammar: Each entry's `summary` is a selection rule, not a description. Format: 'Pick for <content shape>. Skip if <reason → alternative>'. Read it as a decision aid: if the source content matches the Pick clause and does not match a Skip clause, the template fits.

thinking.label: Thinking — Research background / problem / theory / significance
thinking.fits: Use when the figure answers 'why does this work matter?' rather than 'how does the model run?'. The figure presents a fixed set of conceptual panels or quadrants, not a sequential flow.

method.label: Method — Model architecture / mechanism / formula / assumptions
method.fits: Use when the figure explains the internal structure of a single model, algorithm, or mechanism. Often shows inputs → mechanism block → outputs, layered components, or a formula grid.

workflow.label: Workflow — Full-paper data → method → result pipeline
workflow.fits: Use when the figure traces the whole paper from raw data through preprocessing / multiple methods / validation to results. Usually horizontal or circular flow with explicit stage transitions.

ansoff_matrix.label: Ansoff Matrix
ansoff_matrix.summary: Pick for a 2×2 thinking-route framework along two orthogonal conceptual axes (e.g. 'existing vs new' on each axis). Skip if the four cells are not symmetric or do not share a single classification logic — use bcg_matrix for portfolio quadrants, or feature_matrix_table for arbitrary row × column comparisons.

bcg_matrix.label: BCG Matrix
bcg_matrix.summary: Pick for a 2×2 portfolio-style thinking-route where each cell carries a named persona (Star / Cash Cow / Question Mark / Dog or similar). Skip if axes are continuous data not categorical (use scatter_chart in paper2ppt charts) or if cells need richer text per cell (use feature_matrix_table).

chevron_process.label: Chevron Process
chevron_process.summary: Pick for a 3–6 stage horizontal workflow with strong directional momentum (chevron arrows merging into the next). Skip for stages that must show parallelism (use twin-track equivalent) or that loop back (use cycle_diagram).

client_server_flow.label: Client–Server Flow
client_server_flow.summary: Pick for a method-route where data crosses a labeled boundary between two principal blocks (e.g. data layer ↔ model layer, sensor ↔ algorithm, user side ↔ server side) and request/response arrows carry meaning. Skip if the figure has more than two principal blocks (use module_composition) or no directional exchange (use feature_matrix_table).

cycle_diagram.label: Cycle Diagram
cycle_diagram.summary: Pick for a 3–8 stage workflow that explicitly loops (feedback / iteration). Skip for one-shot linear pipelines (use process_flow or chevron_process).

feature_matrix_table.label: Feature Matrix Table
feature_matrix_table.summary: Pick for a row × column thinking-route comparing N approaches across M features, where every cell carries a verdict (✓ / ✗ / partial) or a short string. Skip for purely visual contrasts (use venn_diagram for set overlap, ansoff_matrix for orthogonal axis classification).

icon_grid.label: Icon Grid
icon_grid.summary: Pick for 4–9 conceptually parallel ideas where each gets one icon + one short label + one supporting sentence. Skip for ordered or causal sequences (use chevron_process / process_flow) and for content where the icons would be decorative rather than disambiguating (use vertical_list).

layered_architecture.label: Layered Architecture
layered_architecture.summary: Pick for a method-route diagram with 3–6 horizontally stacked layers, each named (e.g. data layer / feature layer / model layer / output layer). Skip for non-layered modular structure (use module_composition) or for sequential timeline structure (use pipeline_with_stages).

method-formula-grid.label: Formula Grid
method-formula-grid.summary: Pick for a method-route that shows 2–6 named formulas / equations as panels in a grid, each panel with one equation + 1-line meaning. Skip if there is only one central formula (write it inline in the slide body) or if formulas are sequential steps with intermediate text (use vertical_list).

module_composition.label: Module Composition
module_composition.summary: Pick for a method-route where 3–6 modular components are wired together by arrows (e.g. encoder + attention + decoder; data-loader + feature-bank + ranker). Skip for strict 2-block client-server style (use client_server_flow) or for a linear pipeline with no intra-module arrows (use pipeline_with_stages).

org_chart.label: Org / Hierarchy Chart
org_chart.summary: Pick for an explicit hierarchy / taxonomy of a method's parts (e.g. loss = a + b + c, where a / b / c each have children). Skip for non-tree relationships (use module_composition) and for outline-style enumerated tiers (use pyramid_chart or vertical_list).

pipeline_with_stages.label: Pipeline with Stages
pipeline_with_stages.summary: Pick for a workflow-route showing data → preprocessing → model → result with clearly named stage boxes and inter-stage labeled arrows. The whole-paper-thinking default. Skip if stages loop (use cycle_diagram) or if the diagram should emphasise momentum (use chevron_process).

process_flow.label: Process Flow
process_flow.summary: Pick for a generic workflow with 3–8 stages connected by arrows, where the stage boxes hold a 1-line description and the arrows may carry transition labels. Skip if you specifically want chevron momentum (use chevron_process) or layered structure (use layered_architecture).

progress_bar_chart.label: Progress Bar Chart
progress_bar_chart.summary: Pick for a workflow showing N tasks each with current completion % (e.g. ablation pieces done / pending; calibration phases). Skip for ordered timeline with dates (use project_schedule_table) or for steps that are categorical not numeric (use process_flow).

project_schedule_table.label: Project Schedule Table
project_schedule_table.summary: Pick for a proposal / workflow timeline with dated phases and milestones (Gantt-like). Skip for purely conceptual workflow without dates (use process_flow) or for a research-design overview (use pipeline_with_stages).

pyramid_chart.label: Pyramid Chart
pyramid_chart.summary: Pick for a tiered thinking-route where each layer subsumes / supports the layer below (Maslow / Bloom / data-information-knowledge-wisdom / micro-meso-macro). Skip for org structure with siblings at the same tier (use org_chart) or for non-hierarchical layering of architecture parts (use layered_architecture).

venn_diagram.label: Venn Diagram
venn_diagram.summary: Pick for a thinking-route highlighting set overlap (2–3 sets), especially when the contribution sits at the intersection of fields / methods / disciplines. Skip for more than 3 sets (use feature_matrix_table) and for parallel concepts that don't overlap (use icon_grid).

vertical_list.label: Vertical Step List
vertical_list.summary: Pick for an enumerated 3–6 step method-route where each step is a single line of explanation (no formula panel). Skip if any step needs a full-width formula panel (use method-formula-grid) or if the flow is horizontal with arrows (use pipeline_with_stages).
